skill.name: audit
skill.description: Audits codebases for technical debt, type issues, architecture problems, TODOs, and code smells using systematic file review.
skill.summary: Run a systematic codebase audit for quality, architecture, type, and technical debt issues.
skill.author: Cygnusfear

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external command patterns and weak cryptography patterns in SKILL.md. Manual review confirmed only local command execution guidance for type checking and linting; the weak cryptography matches are false positives in prose and markdown examples.

medium_findings.title: External Local Command Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to run local validation commands such as TypeScript checks and lint scripts. This is legitimate for a code audit, but project scripts can execute arbitrary local code, so users should run it only in trusted repositories.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines directly name local commands used for validation. The context is legitimate developer tooling, but command execution risk remains because project scripts can run local code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Detected As Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The flagged backtick locations are markdown formatting for a report path, type names, and a fenced markdown example. Line 41 was reported twice by static analysis, but no Ruby or shell backtick execution is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows these are documentation backticks, not executable code. There is no shell interpolation or runtime context in the skill file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain the skill description, an audit report placeholder, and a cross-file pattern placeholder. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing API, or security-sensitive crypto usage appears in the file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence lines do not reference MD5, SHA1, DES, or any cryptographic operation. The scanner likely matched incidental text or markdown structure.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Local Command Execution Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the agent to run local validation commands such as TypeScript checks and lint scripts. This is legitimate for a code audit, but project scripts can execute arbitrary local code, so users should run it only in trusted repositories.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines directly name local commands used for validation. The context is legitimate developer tooling, but command execution risk remains because project scripts can run local code.

content.value_statement: Large codebases hide TODOs, type gaps, and architecture problems across many files. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through a structured read-only audit.
actual_capabilities: Builds a file-by-file audit workflow for a requested codebase scope.
Checks for TODOs, FIXMEs, deprecated code, type issues, and code smells.
Prompts use of TypeScript, linting, and build checks when project tools are available.
Organizes findings by severity, category, file, and cross-file pattern.
Produces a structured markdown audit report for review.
limitations: It does not fix code or create implementation plans.
It depends on repository access and available local tooling.
It may miss runtime issues that require tests, traffic, or production data.
It can ask to run local commands, so use trusted repositories.

use_cases.title: Repository Maintenance Audit
use_cases.description: Find technical debt, TODOs, and stale patterns before a maintenance cycle.

use_cases.title: Pre-Refactor Assessment
use_cases.description: Map architectural risks and repeated code smells before changing a module.

use_cases.title: TypeScript Quality Review
use_cases.description: Identify weak type usage, casts, and missing annotations across selected files.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Code Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a small repository or folder.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this repository for TODOs, deprecated code, type issues, and code smells. Create a structured report only.

prompt_templates.title: Scoped Module Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit the src/components directory for architecture issues, duplicated patterns, and type problems. Include file and line references.

prompt_templates.title: Pre-Release Quality Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a read-only audit before release. Include automated check summaries, severity counts, and the top ten findings.

prompt_templates.title: Architecture Debt Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit the codebase for cross-file architecture problems, coupling, circular dependencies, inconsistent patterns, and systemic technical debt.

output_examples.input: Audit the backend package for code quality issues.
output_examples.output: A markdown report with issue counts, critical findings, category sections, affected files, and automated tool summaries.

output_examples.input: Review this TypeScript app for type problems and TODOs.
output: Summary of files audited and total issues.
Grouped findings for type issues, TODOs, deprecations, and code smells.
Cross-file patterns that need human review.

output_examples.input: Check this module before a refactor.
output_examples.output: A focused audit that identifies architectural risks, repeated patterns, and high-priority files for later planning.

best_practices: Define the audit scope before starting to avoid unrelated findings.
Run it in a trusted checkout where local commands are acceptable.
Review the report before creating fixes or implementation plans.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as an automatic fixer because it is designed for read-only analysis.
Do not skip generated files or vendored files without stating the exclusion.
Do not treat severity labels as final without maintainer review.

faq.question: Does this skill edit code?
faq.answer: No. It instructs the agent to identify and report issues without changing files.

faq.question: Can it run project commands?
faq.answer: Yes. It may ask to run type checks, linting, or build commands when those tools exist.

faq.question: Is it only for TypeScript projects?
faq.answer: No. It includes TypeScript checks, but the manual audit process applies to many codebases.

faq.question: What output does it create?
faq.answer: It creates a structured markdown audit report with findings, severities, and categories.

faq.question: Does it provide recommendations?
faq.answer: No. The skill focuses on finding and reporting problems, not planning fixes.

faq.question: Is it safe for untrusted repositories?
faq.answer: Use caution. The skill may run local project commands, and untrusted scripts can execute arbitrary code.
